_comment: Field classification for the 10 journals in the corpus. Kept as DATA, not
code, because it is an editorial judgement about disciplinary culture and a
user may reasonably disagree. Edit here; both views pick it up.
'classics'   = general classical-studies journals, where ancient philosophy
sits alongside literature, history, papyrology, epigraphy.
'philosophy' = philosophy journals, including ancient-philosophy specialist
journals housed in philosophy rather than classics.
Arguable cases, stated openly rather than hidden:
Phronesis — the flagship ancient-philosophy journal, read across both
camps. Filed under philosophy (philosophy-department milieu), but a
classicist might reasonably file it the other way. It is also the
single largest source (26,703 citations, 29% of the corpus), so this
one assignment moves the philosophy/classics comparison more than any
other. Treat any philosophy-vs-classics contrast as partly a claim
about where Phronesis belongs.
Methexis, Revue de Philosophie Ancienne — ancient-philosophy specialists;
same reasoning as Phronesis, smaller volume so lower stakes.
Revue Internationale de Philosophie, Les Etudes philosophiques,
Archivio di Filosofia — general philosophy journals that publish
ancient material; unambiguously philosophy.
